test_cases.description: Verifies that a workspace slug meeting all format rules (3-64 chars, starts with lowercase letter, only lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, no trailing hyphen) is accepted and stored.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
No workspace with slug 'abc' or 'my-workspace-123' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object containing slug='my-workspace-123' and all eight schema fields present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'my-workspace-123', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.slug == 'my-workspace-123'
assert 'id' in response.body
assert 'owner_user_id' in response.body
assert 'created_at' in response.body
assert 'updated_at' in response.body

test_cases.description: Verifies that a slug failing format rules (e.g. contains uppercase letters) is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<non-empty description>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'Invalid-Slug!', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 400
assert response.body.error.code == 400
assert len(response.body.error.message) > 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that submitting a slug that already exists in the workspaces table returns HTTP 409.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A workspace with slug 'existing-slug' already exists in the database
A valid user API key exists in the database
test_cases.expected: HTTP 409 with JSON error envelope {"error":{"code":409,"message":"<non-empty description>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'existing-slug', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 409
assert response.body.error.code == 409
assert len(response.body.error.message) > 0

test_cases.description: Verifies that git_url in HTTPS format is accepted and stored verbatim without reachability checks.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
No workspace with slug 'test-https-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object where git_url is 'https://github.com/org/repo.git' exactly as submitted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'test-https-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.git_url == 'https://github.com/org/repo.git'

test_cases.description: Verifies that git_url in SCP-style SSH format (git@host:path) is accepted and stored verbatim.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
No workspace with slug 'test-ssh-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object where git_url is 'git@github.com:org/repo.git' exactly as submitted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'test-ssh-ws', git_url:'git@github.com:org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.git_url == 'git@github.com:org/repo.git'

test_cases.description: Verifies that git_url using an unsupported scheme (e.g. ssh://) is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<non-empty description>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for scheme in ['ssh://github.com/org/repo.git', 'git://github.com/org/repo.git', 'http://github.com/org/repo.git', 'ftp://github.com/org/repo.git']:
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'bad-ws', git_url:scheme})
  assert response.status == 400
  assert response.body.error.code == 400

test_cases.description: Verifies that a git_url exceeding 2048 characters is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<non-empty description>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
long_url = 'https://github.com/org/' + 'a' * 2028 + '.git'  // total > 2048
assert len(long_url) > 2048
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'long-url-ws', git_url:long_url})
assert response.status == 400
assert response.body.error.code == 400

test_cases.description: Verifies that omitting branch or providing null results in null branch in the returned workspace object.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
No workspace with slug 'no-branch-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object where branch is null
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'no-branch-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.branch == null

response2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'null-branch-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch:null})
assert response2.status == 201
assert response2.body.branch == null

test_cases.description: Verifies that a valid branch string (<=255 chars, no ASCII whitespace) is accepted and stored verbatim.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
No workspace with slug 'branch-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object where branch is 'feature/my-branch'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'branch-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch:'feature/my-branch'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.branch == 'feature/my-branch'

test_cases.description: Verifies that a branch string exceeding 255 characters is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
long_branch = 'a' * 256
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'long-branch-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch:long_branch})
assert response.status == 400
assert response.body.error.code == 400

test_cases.description: Verifies that a branch string containing ASCII whitespace characters is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists in the database
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope for each whitespace variant (space, tab, newline, CR)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for branch_val in ['my branch', 'my\tbranch', 'my\nbranch', 'my\rbranch']:
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'ws-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', branch:branch_val})
  assert response.status == 400
  assert response.body.error.code == 400

test_cases.description: Verifies that omitting team_id or providing null results in null team_id in the returned workspace object.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and teams tables
A valid user API key exists in the database
No workspace with slug 'no-team-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object where team_id is null
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'no-team-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.team_id == null

test_cases.description: Verifies that providing a valid active team_id causes a direct SQL lookup and stores the team_id in the workspace.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and teams tables
A valid active team with id 'team-abc-123' exists in the teams table
A valid user API key exists in the database
No workspace with slug 'team-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object where team_id is 'team-abc-123'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'team-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', team_id:'team-abc-123'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.team_id == 'team-abc-123'

test_cases.description: Verifies that providing a team_id that does not exist in the teams table returns HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and teams tables
No team with id 'nonexistent-team' exists in the teams table
A valid user API key exists in the database
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope

test_cases.description: Verifies that a valid POST /api/v1/workspaces request with user API key creates the workspace and returns HTTP 201 with all eight schema fields.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists for user with id 'user-001'
No workspace with slug 'new-workspace' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object containing id, slug, git_url, branch (null), team_id (null), owner_user_id='user-001', created_at, updated_at; updated_at equals created_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'new-workspace', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.slug == 'new-workspace'
assert response.body.owner_user_id == 'user-001'
assert response.body.updated_at == response.body.created_at
for field in ['id','slug','git_url','branch','team_id','owner_user_id','created_at','updated_at']:
  assert field in response.body

test_cases.description: Verifies that an admin token is rejected with HTTP 403 when attempting to create a workspace.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid admin token exists in the system
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<non-empty description>"}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'admin-created-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'}, headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_at_<admin_token>'})
assert response.status == 403
assert response.body.error.code == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that a workspace token is rejected with HTTP 403 when attempting to create a workspace.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A valid non-expired, non-revoked workspace token exists for some workspace
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'wt-created-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'}, headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>'})
assert response.status == 403
assert response.body.error.code == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that a user API key holder listing workspaces receives only their own workspaces ordered by created_at ASC, id ASC.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
User 'user-001' owns workspaces with slugs 'ws-a' and 'ws-b'
User 'user-002' owns workspace with slug 'ws-c'
A valid user API key exists for 'user-001'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array containing only ws-a and ws-b (not ws-c), ordered by created_at ASC then id ASC
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_key_<user001_key>'})
assert response.status == 200
assert isinstance(response.body, list)
assert all(ws.owner_user_id == 'user-001' for ws in response.body)
assert 'ws-c' not in [ws.slug for ws in response.body]
assert response.body == sorted(response.body, key=lambda w: (w.created_at, w.id))

test_cases.description: Verifies that an admin token listing workspaces receives all workspaces ordered by created_at ASC, id ASC.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
Multiple workspaces owned by different users exist in the database
A valid admin token exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array containing all workspaces from all users, ordered by created_at ASC then id ASC
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_at_<admin_token>'})
assert response.status == 200
assert isinstance(response.body, list)
assert len(response.body) == total_workspace_count_in_db
assert response.body == sorted(response.body, key=lambda w: (w.created_at, w.id))

test_cases.description: Verifies that a workspace token is rejected with HTTP 403 when calling GET /api/v1/workspaces.

test_cases.description: Verifies that a user with no workspaces receives an empty JSON array from GET /api/v1/workspaces.

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:

assert response.body == []

test_cases.description: Verifies that workspace owner or admin can get workspace details by slug returning all eight schema fields.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A workspace with slug 'target-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
Valid user API key for 'user-001' and valid admin token exist
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with full workspace object containing all eight fields for 'target-ws'

test_cases.description: Verifies that a valid workspace token scoped to the same workspace returns the full workspace object without field redaction.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace with slug 'scoped-ws' exists with a known id
A valid non-expired, non-revoked workspace token scoped to 'scoped-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with full workspace object containing all eight fields, identical to what owner/admin callers receive
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/scoped-ws', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<token_id>_<secret>'})
assert response.status == 200
assert response.body.slug == 'scoped-ws'
for field in ['id','slug','git_url','branch','team_id','owner_user_id','created_at','updated_at']:
  assert field in response.body

test_cases.description: Verifies that a non-existent slug returns HTTP 404 regardless of caller type (owner, admin, or workspace token).
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
No workspace with slug 'ghost-ws' exists
Valid user API key, admin token, and workspace token are available
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON error envelope for all caller types
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for auth_header in ['Bearer af_key_<valid_key>', 'Bearer af_at_<admin_token>']:
  response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/ghost-ws', headers={'Authorization':auth_header})
  assert response.status == 404
  assert response.body.error.code == 404

test_cases.description: Verifies that a non-owner user API key gets HTTP 403 when accessing a workspace they do not own (that exists).
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A workspace with slug 'others-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
A valid user API key exists for 'user-002' (not the owner)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/others-ws', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_key_<user002_key>'})
assert response.status == 403
assert response.body.error.code == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that a valid workspace token scoped to a different workspace returns HTTP 403 on GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
Two workspaces exist: 'workspace-a' and 'workspace-b'
A valid non-expired, non-revoked workspace token is scoped to 'workspace-a' only
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope due to workspace_id mismatch
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/workspace-b', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<token_scoped_to_workspace_a>'})
assert response.status == 403
assert response.body.error.code == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that the workspace object returned by all workspace endpoints contains exactly the eight defined fields with correct types.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A workspace with slug 'schema-check-ws' exists
A valid user API key for the workspace owner exists
test_cases.expected: Response body contains exactly: id (string/UUID), slug (string), git_url (string), branch (string or null), team_id (string or null), owner_user_id (string), created_at (ISO 8601 string), updated_at (ISO 8601 string); no additional or missing fields
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/schema-check-ws')
assert response.status == 200
expected_fields = {'id','slug','git_url','branch','team_id','owner_user_id','created_at','updated_at'}
assert set(response.body.keys()) == expected_fields
assert isinstance(response.body.id, str)
assert isinstance(response.body.slug, str)
assert isinstance(response.body.git_url, str)
assert response.body.branch is None or isinstance(response.body.branch, str)
assert response.body.team_id is None or isinstance(response.body.team_id, str)
assert isinstance(response.body.owner_user_id, str)
assert is_iso8601(response.body.created_at)
assert is_iso8601(response.body.updated_at)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the workspace object schema is identical for owner, admin, and workspace token callers without any field redaction.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'uniform-ws' exists with all fields populated
Valid credentials for owner, admin, and a scoped workspace token all exist
test_cases.expected: All three caller types receive identical JSON field sets and values
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
owner_resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/uniform-ws', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_key_<owner>'})
admin_resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/uniform-ws', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_at_<admin>'})
token_resp = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/uniform-ws', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<scoped_token>'})
assert set(owner_resp.body.keys()) == set(admin_resp.body.keys()) == set(token_resp.body.keys())
assert owner_resp.body == admin_resp.body == token_resp.body

test_cases.description: Verifies that updated_at is set equal to created_at in the INSERT statement and reflected in the HTTP 201 response.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
No workspace with slug 'ts-eq-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 response with updated_at equal to created_at in the response body
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'ts-eq-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.updated_at == response.body.created_at

test_cases.description: Verifies that a valid token creation request from the workspace owner returns HTTP 201 with the full token creation response including plaintext token.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace with slug 'token-create-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
A valid user API key exists for 'user-001'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with response containing token (af_wt_ prefixed), token_id (8 chars), label, expires_at (ISO 8601), created_at (ISO 8601)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/token-create-ws/tokens', body={label:'my-token', expires:30})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.token.startswith('af_wt_')
assert len(response.body.token_id) == 8
assert is_base62(response.body.token_id)
assert response.body.label == 'my-token'
assert is_iso8601(response.body.expires_at)
assert is_iso8601(response.body.created_at)
// Verify token format: af_wt_<8chars>_<32chars>
assert re.match(r'^af_wt_[0-9A-Za-z]{8}_[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$', response.body.token)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the plaintext token is returned in the token field and the secret is stored only as SHA-256 hash in the database.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'hash-check-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
Valid user API key for 'user-001'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; token field contains full plaintext; workspace_tokens table row contains SHA-256 hash of secret, not plaintext
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/hash-check-ws/tokens', body={})
assert response.status == 201
token_str = response.body.token  // e.g. af_wt_XXXXXXXX_YYYYYY...
parts = token_str.split('_')  // ['af', 'wt', '<token_id>', '<secret>']
secret = parts[3]
// Query SQLite directly: SELECT secret_hash FROM workspace_tokens WHERE token_id = parts[2]
db_row = db.query('SELECT secret_hash FROM workspace_tokens WHERE token_id = ?', parts[2])
assert db_row.secret_hash == sha256_hex(secret)
assert db_row.secret_hash != secret  // plaintext never stored

test_cases.description: Verifies that omitting expires defaults to 30 days and expires=0 produces null expires_at.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'expiry-ws' exists with valid owner API key
test_cases.expected: When expires is omitted: expires_at is set to created_at + 30 days. When expires=0: expires_at is null.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// Test default (omitted expires)
resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/expiry-ws/tokens', body={})
assert resp1.status == 201
expected_expiry = parse_iso8601(resp1.body.created_at) + 30_days
assert abs(parse_iso8601(resp1.body.expires_at) - expected_expiry) < 1_second

// Test expires=0 => null
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/expiry-ws/tokens', body={expires:0})
assert resp2.status == 201
assert resp2.body.expires_at == null

test_cases.description: Verifies that an invalid expires value (not 0, 30, 60, or 90) is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'bad-expires-ws' exists with valid owner API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope for any expires value not in {0, 30, 60, 90}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for bad_expires in [1, 29, 31, 45, 91, 100, -1]:
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/bad-expires-ws/tokens', body={expires:bad_expires})
  assert response.status == 400
  assert response.body.error.code == 400

test_cases.description: Verifies that empty string label is normalized to null, omitted label is null, and non-empty label is stored as-is.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'label-ws' exists with valid owner API key
test_cases.expected: Empty string label normalized to null; omitted label is null; 'my-label' stored as 'my-label'

test_cases.description: Verifies that a label exceeding 128 characters is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'long-label-ws' exists with valid owner API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
long_label = 'a' * 129
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/long-label-ws/tokens', body={label:long_label})
assert response.status == 400
assert response.body.error.code == 400

test_cases.description: Verifies that non-owner users and workspace token callers are rejected with HTTP 403 on POST .../tokens.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'restricted-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
Valid API key for 'user-002' (non-owner) and a valid workspace token for 'restricted-ws' exist
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 for non-owner API key; HTTP 403 for workspace token caller
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
// Non-owner user
resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/restricted-ws/tokens', body={}, headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_key_<user002_key>'})
assert resp1.status == 403
assert resp1.body.error.code == 403

// Workspace token
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/restricted-ws/tokens', body={}, headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<token>'})
assert resp2.status == 403
assert resp2.body.error.code == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that POST .../tokens for a non-existent slug returns HTTP 404.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
No workspace with slug 'ghost-token-ws' exists
A valid admin token exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/ghost-token-ws/tokens', body={})
assert response.status == 404
assert response.body.error.code == 404

test_cases.description: Verifies that GET .../tokens returns all tokens (including expired/revoked) ordered by created_at ASC, id ASC, with metadata only (no secret).
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'list-tokens-ws' exists with 3 tokens: one active, one expired, one revoked
Valid owner API key for this workspace
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with JSON array of 3 token metadata objects, ordered by created_at ASC then id ASC, each having token_id, label, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at; no secret field
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/list-tokens-ws/tokens')
assert response.status == 200
assert len(response.body) == 3
assert response.body == sorted(response.body, key=lambda t: (t.created_at, t.id))
for token in response.body:
  for field in ['token_id', 'label', 'created_at', 'expires_at', 'revoked_at']:
    assert field in token
  assert 'token' not in token
  assert 'secret' not in token

test_cases.description: Verifies that a workspace token is rejected with HTTP 403 when calling GET .../tokens.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'no-wt-list-ws' exists with a valid scoped workspace token
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/no-wt-list-ws/tokens', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<token>'})

test_cases.description: Verifies that listing tokens for a non-existent workspace slug returns HTTP 404.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
No workspace with slug 'nope-ws' exists
A valid admin token exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/nope-ws/tokens')
assert response.status == 404
assert response.body.error.code == 404

test_cases.description: Verifies that a non-owner user API key is rejected with HTTP 403 when listing workspace tokens.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'protected-tokens-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
Valid API key for 'user-002' (non-owner) exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/protected-tokens-ws/tokens', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_key_<user002_key>'})
assert response.status == 403
assert response.body.error.code == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that a workspace with no tokens returns an empty JSON array from GET .../tokens.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'empty-tokens-ws' exists with no tokens
Valid owner API key exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 with empty JSON array []
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/empty-tokens-ws/tokens')
assert response.status == 200
assert response.body == []

test_cases.description: Verifies that DELETE .../tokens/:token_id revokes a valid token and returns HTTP 204 with no body.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'revoke-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
A non-revoked token with token_id 'tok00001' belongs to 'revoke-ws'
Valid owner API key for 'user-001'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 with empty response body; workspace_tokens table row for tok00001 has non-null revoked_at
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/revoke-ws/tokens/tok00001')
assert response.status == 204
assert response.body == '' or response.body is None
// Verify in DB
row = db.query('SELECT revoked_at FROM workspace_tokens WHERE token_id = ?', 'tok00001')
assert row.revoked_at is not null

test_cases.description: Verifies that revoking an already-revoked token is idempotent and returns HTTP 204.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'idempotent-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
A token with token_id 'tok00002' belongs to 'idempotent-ws' and is already revoked (revoked_at is set)
Valid owner API key for 'user-001'
test_cases.expected: HTTP 204 with empty response body; no error returned for already-revoked token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/idempotent-ws/tokens/tok00002')
assert response.status == 204
assert response.body == '' or response.body is None

test_cases.description: Verifies that DELETE with a token_id not found in workspace_tokens returns HTTP 404.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'del-404-ws' exists
No token with id 'nonexist1' exists in workspace_tokens
Valid owner API key exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/del-404-ws/tokens/nonexist1')
assert response.status == 404
assert response.body.error.code == 404

test_cases.description: Verifies that a token_id belonging to a different workspace returns HTTP 404 (information hiding).
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
Workspace 'ws-alpha' and 'ws-beta' both exist
Token 'tok-beta-1' belongs to 'ws-beta'
Valid owner API key for 'ws-alpha' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON error envelope; cross-workspace token_id treated as not found
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/ws-alpha/tokens/tok-beta-1')
assert response.status == 404
assert response.body.error.code == 404

test_cases.description: Verifies that non-owner users and workspace token callers are rejected with HTTP 403 on DELETE .../tokens/:token_id.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'protected-del-ws' exists owned by 'user-001'
A token 'tok-active' belongs to this workspace
Valid API key for 'user-002' (non-owner) and a workspace token exist
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope for non-owner and workspace token callers
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp1 = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/protected-del-ws/tokens/tok-active', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_key_<user002_key>'})
assert resp1.status == 403
assert resp1.body.error.code == 403

resp2 = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/protected-del-ws/tokens/tok-active', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<token>'})
assert resp2.status == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that DELETE .../tokens/:token_id for a non-existent workspace slug returns HTTP 404.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
No workspace with slug 'missing-ws' exists
A valid admin token exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 404 with JSON error envelope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/missing-ws/tokens/sometoken1')
assert response.status == 404
assert response.body.error.code == 404

test_cases.description: Verifies that the generated workspace token matches the format af_wt_<8 base62 chars>_<32 base62 chars>.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'format-check-ws' exists with a valid owner API key
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201 with token field matching regex ^af_wt_[0-9A-Za-z]{8}_[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/format-check-ws/tokens', body={})
assert response.status == 201
assert re.fullmatch(r'af_wt_[0-9A-Za-z]{8}_[0-9A-Za-z]{32}', response.body.token)
assert re.fullmatch(r'[0-9A-Za-z]{8}', response.body.token_id)

test_cases.description: Verifies that the secret is stored as SHA-256 hash only and the plaintext is never persisted in workspace_tokens.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'hash-store-ws' exists with valid owner API key
test_cases.expected: The workspace_tokens row's secret hash column contains SHA-256(secret), not the plaintext secret
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/hash-store-ws/tokens', body={})
token_str = response.body.token
secret = token_str.split('_')[3]
token_id = response.body.token_id
row = db.query('SELECT * FROM workspace_tokens WHERE token_id = ?', token_id)
assert row.secret_hash == hex(sha256(secret.encode()))
assert row.secret_hash != secret
// No column should contain the raw secret string
assert secret not in str(row)

test_cases.description: Verifies that expired or revoked workspace tokens are rejected with HTTP 401 by the auth middleware before the handler runs.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'auth-check-ws' exists
An expired workspace token (expires_at in the past) and a revoked workspace token (revoked_at set) both exist for this workspace
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401 for expired token; HTTP 401 for revoked token; both rejected before handler executes

test_cases.description: Verifies that a workspace token belonging to a blocked user is rejected with HTTP 401 by the auth middleware.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces, workspace_tokens, and users tables
A workspace 'blocked-user-ws' exists
A valid non-expired, non-revoked workspace token exists for a user whose account is blocked
test_cases.expected: HTTP 401 returned by auth middleware; handler never executes
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/blocked-user-ws', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<blocked_user_token>'})
assert response.status == 401

test_cases.description: Verifies the complete access control matrix is enforced across all endpoint and caller combinations.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with all required tables
Workspaces, tokens, owner user, non-owner user, admin token, and workspace tokens are all present in the database
test_cases.expected: Admin token: 403 on POST /api/v1/workspaces, 200 on all others. Non-owner user: 403 on GET/:slug for others' workspaces, 403 on token management for others' workspaces. Workspace token: 403 on all except GET/:slug for own workspace.

test_cases.description: Verifies that the handler compares workspace_id from context against the slug-resolved workspace id and returns HTTP 403 on mismatch.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
Two workspaces: 'ws-one' (id=uuid-1) and 'ws-two' (id=uuid-2) exist
A workspace token is scoped to 'ws-one' (workspace_id=uuid-1)
test_cases.expected: HTTP 403 because context workspace_id (uuid-1) does not match ws-two's id (uuid-2)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/ws-two', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_wt_<token_scoped_to_ws_one>'})
assert response.status == 403
assert response.body.error.code == 403

test_cases.description: Verifies that unknown extra fields in the POST request body are silently ignored and do not cause HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
No workspace with slug 'extra-fields-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; unknown fields are silently ignored; workspace is created normally
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'extra-fields-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git', unknown_field:'ignored', another_extra:42})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.slug == 'extra-fields-ws'

test_cases.description: Verifies that POST endpoints process valid JSON body regardless of whether Content-Type header is present.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
No workspace with slug 'no-content-type-ws' exists
test_cases.expected: HTTP 201; request is processed normally without Content-Type: application/json header

property_tests.description: Verifies that no two workspace records in the workspaces table ever share the same slug value.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate N random valid slugs (3-64 chars, starts with letter, alphanumeric+hyphens, no trailing hyphen); attempt to create workspaces with each slug including deliberate duplicates
property_tests.invariant_check: After all creation attempts: query SELECT slug, COUNT(*) FROM workspaces GROUP BY slug HAVING COUNT(*) > 1; assert the result set is empty. For each duplicate attempt, assert the response was HTTP 409.

property_tests.description: Verifies that for every record in workspace_tokens, the plaintext secret is never stored — only its SHA-256 hash.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any set of workspace token creation responses, collect all (token_id, plaintext_secret) pairs from response bodies
property_tests.invariant_check: For each collected pair: query SELECT * FROM workspace_tokens WHERE token_id = ?; assert no column value equals the plaintext secret; assert the secret_hash column equals hex(sha256(plaintext_secret.encode('utf-8'))); repeat for all created tokens

property_tests.description: Verifies that the plaintext token string is returned exactly once (in the creation response) and never exposed by any subsequent endpoint.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any created workspace token, collect the plaintext token string from the POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens response
property_tests.invariant_check: Call GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens; assert no object in the array contains a 'token' field or any field matching the full plaintext token string or the raw secret substring. The token field must not appear in list responses under any key.

property_tests.description: Verifies that expired or revoked workspace tokens are always rejected with HTTP 401 by the auth middleware before any handler executes, for any endpoint.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any workspace token that has expires_at in the past or revoked_at set, generate requests to all six endpoint routes
property_tests.invariant_check: For each request with an expired/revoked token across all endpoints: assert response.status == 401. The assertion must hold for POST /api/v1/workspaces, GET /api/v1/workspaces, GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug, POST .../tokens, GET .../tokens, DELETE .../tokens/:id.

property_tests.description: Verifies that for any request authenticated by a workspace token targeting GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug, a workspace_id mismatch always produces HTTP 403.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any two distinct workspaces W1 and W2, and a valid workspace token T scoped to W1, issue GET /api/v1/workspaces/<W2.slug> with token T
property_tests.invariant_check: Assert response.status == 403 for every such mismatch combination. Confirm the handler compares context workspace_id (W1.id) against resolved workspace id (W2.id) and never allows access.

property_tests.description: Verifies that for every workspace record inserted, updated_at is set equal to created_at at creation time.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any set of valid workspace creation requests
property_tests.invariant_check: After each successful POST /api/v1/workspaces (HTTP 201): assert response.body.updated_at == response.body.created_at. Also query the database directly: SELECT created_at, updated_at FROM workspaces WHERE id = ?; assert db_row.updated_at == db_row.created_at.

property_tests.description: Verifies that token_id generation retries are bounded at exactly 3 and the handler never enters an unbounded loop.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Mock crypto/rand to always produce a colliding token_id; instrument the retry loop with a counter; call POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens
property_tests.invariant_check: Assert retry_counter <= 3 after the handler returns. Assert that when all 3 attempts collide, the handler returns HTTP 500. Assert that on the first successful non-colliding attempt (mocked to succeed on attempt K <= 3), retry_counter == K and HTTP 201 is returned. The loop must not execute more than 3 iterations under any condition.

property_tests.description: Verifies that DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id is idempotent — always returns HTTP 204 when the token exists and belongs to the workspace, regardless of prior revocation state.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any token that exists and belongs to the target workspace (in any state: active, already-revoked)
property_tests.invariant_check: Issue DELETE once: assert response.status == 204. Issue DELETE again on the same token: assert response.status == 204. The response must be HTTP 204 on both calls. The revoked_at timestamp set on the first successful call must not change on subsequent calls.

property_tests.description: Verifies that list responses from GET /api/v1/workspaces and GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens are always ordered by created_at ASC with id ASC as tiebreaker.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any set of workspaces or tokens with varying created_at timestamps (including some identical timestamps with different ids)
property_tests.invariant_check: For GET /api/v1/workspaces: assert response.body[i].created_at <= response.body[i+1].created_at for all i; when created_at[i] == created_at[i+1], assert response.body[i].id < response.body[i+1].id lexicographically. Apply the same assertion to GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens response array using the token records' created_at and id fields.

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a slug of exactly 2 characters (below minimum) and exactly 65 characters (above maximum) are both rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope for both 2-char slug and 65-char slug
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// 2-char slug (below minimum of 3)
resp1 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'ab', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp1.status == 400
assert resp1.body.error.code == 400

// 65-char slug (above maximum of 64)
slug65 = 'a' + 'b' * 63 + 'c'  // 65 chars total, starts with letter, no trailing hyphen
assert len(slug65) == 65
resp2 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:slug65, git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp2.status == 400
assert resp2.body.error.code == 400

// Boundary: 3-char slug should succeed
resp3 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'abc', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp3.status == 201

// Boundary: 64-char slug should succeed
slug64 = 'a' + 'b' * 63
assert len(slug64) == 64
resp4 = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:slug64, git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert resp4.status == 201

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a slug ending with a hyphen (e.g. 'my-workspace-') is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'my-workspace-', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 400
assert response.body.error.code == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that slugs starting with a digit or hyphen are rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope for slugs starting with digit or hyphen
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
for slug in ['1workspace', '-workspace']:
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:slug, git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
  assert response.status == 400
  assert response.body.error.code == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a git_url of exactly 2049 characters (one over the limit) is rejected with HTTP 400.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope for a 2049-character git_url
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
prefix = 'https://github.com/org/'
filler = 'a' * (2049 - len(prefix) - len('.git'))
long_url = prefix + filler + '.git'
assert len(long_url) == 2049
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'url-boundary-ws', git_url:long_url})
assert response.status == 400
assert response.body.error.code == 400

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a git_url of exactly 2048 characters starting with https:// is accepted.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
No workspace with slug 'url-exact-ws' exists
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 with workspace object; git_url stored verbatim
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
prefix = 'https://github.com/org/'
filler = 'a' * (2048 - len(prefix) - len('.git'))
exact_url = prefix + filler + '.git'
assert len(exact_url) == 2048
response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'url-exact-ws', git_url:exact_url})
assert response.status == 201
assert response.body.git_url == exact_url

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies the system's behavior when branch is an empty string, documenting whether it is stored as empty or treated as null.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
No workspace with slug 'empty-branch-ws' exists
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 if empty string passes whitespace/length validation (stored as-is or normalized); implementation must document the behavior — the test captures and asserts whichever behavior is implemented

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that an unexpected database error during workspace INSERT returns HTTP 500 with no partial record visible.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
A valid user API key exists
Database INSERT is configured to fail (e.g. via mock/stub of the DB layer)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with JSON error envelope; workspaces table contains no record for slug 'db-error-ws'
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// Inject DB failure for INSERT into workspaces
with mock_db_insert_error():
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces', body={slug:'db-error-ws', git_url:'https://github.com/org/repo.git'})
assert response.status == 500
assert response.body.error.code == 500
// Verify no partial record
count = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE slug = ?', 'db-error-ws')
assert count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that when all 3 token_id generation retries result in collision, HTTP 500 is returned and no partial token record is inserted.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'collision-ws' exists with a valid owner API key
crypto/rand is mocked to always produce the same token_id that already exists in workspace_tokens
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with JSON error envelope; exactly 3 generation attempts made; no token record inserted
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
attempt_count = 0
def mock_rand_always_collide():
  global attempt_count
  attempt_count += 1
  return FIXED_COLLIDING_TOKEN_ID

with mock_crypto_rand(mock_rand_always_collide), pre_insert_token(FIXED_COLLIDING_TOKEN_ID):
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/collision-ws/tokens', body={})

assert response.status == 500
assert response.body.error.code == 500
assert attempt_count == 3
// No new token records beyond the pre-existing collision record
count = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspace_tokens WHERE workspace_id = ? AND token_id != ?', workspace_id, FIXED_COLLIDING_TOKEN_ID)
assert count == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that an unexpected database error during token INSERT returns HTTP 500 with no partial token visible.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'db-err-token-ws' exists with valid owner API key
Database INSERT into workspace_tokens is configured to fail (non-collision error)
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with JSON error envelope; workspace_tokens table contains no new record
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
initial_count = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspace_tokens WHERE workspace_id = ?', workspace_id)
with mock_db_insert_error_for(table='workspace_tokens'):
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/db-err-token-ws/tokens', body={})
assert response.status == 500
assert response.body.error.code == 500
final_count = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspace_tokens WHERE workspace_id = ?', workspace_id)
assert initial_count == final_count

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that token creation is unbounded — no maximum token count per workspace — and multiple tokens may share the same label.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'many-tokens-ws' exists with valid owner API key
Multiple tokens (e.g. 10) already exist for the workspace, some sharing labels
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 201 regardless of existing token count; duplicate label is accepted

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a non-owner user querying a non-existent slug receives HTTP 404 (not HTTP 403), preserving information hiding.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces table
No workspace with slug 'nonexistent-ws' exists
A valid user API key for 'user-002' (not an owner of any workspace named 'nonexistent-ws') exists
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 404 (not HTTP 403); non-existence is treated uniformly for all callers
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
response = GET('/api/v1/workspaces/nonexistent-ws', headers={'Authorization':'Bearer af_key_<user002_key>'})
assert response.status == 404  // NOT 403
assert response.body.error.code == 404

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that an unexpected database error during token revocation UPDATE returns HTTP 500 and revoked_at remains unchanged.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'revoke-err-ws' exists with token 'tok-revoke-err' (not yet revoked)
Database UPDATE on workspace_tokens is configured to fail
Valid owner API key exists
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with JSON error envelope; token's revoked_at remains null
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
with mock_db_update_error_for(table='workspace_tokens'):
  response = DELETE('/api/v1/workspaces/revoke-err-ws/tokens/tok-revoke-err')
assert response.status == 500
assert response.body.error.code == 500
row = db.query('SELECT revoked_at FROM workspace_tokens WHERE token_id = ?', 'tok-revoke-err')
assert row.revoked_at is null

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that a crypto/rand read error during token generation propagates as a Go error and results in HTTP 500 with no partial record.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialised with the workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
A workspace 'rand-err-ws' exists with valid owner API key
crypto/rand.Read is mocked to return an error
edge_case_tests.expected: HTTP 500 with JSON error envelope; no token record inserted; no os.Exit or panic
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
with mock_crypto_rand_error():
  response = POST('/api/v1/workspaces/rand-err-ws/tokens', body={})
assert response.status == 500
assert response.body.error.code == 500
count = db.query('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspace_tokens WHERE workspace_id = ?', workspace_id)
assert count == 0

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the end-to-end workspace creation flow using a user API key, validating the full path from auth middleware through DB insert to HTTP 201 response.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /api/v1/workspaces with valid user API key and valid request body including slug, git_url, branch, and team_id
real_components: Echo HTTP server with registered routes
Auth middleware (server_foundation spec 01)
POST /api/v1/workspaces handler
SQLite database with workspaces and teams tables
mockable: teams table lookup (can be real SQLite)
expected_effects: HTTP 201 response returned
Response body contains all eight workspace schema fields
owner_user_id matches the authenticated user's ID
updated_at equals created_at in response body
One new record exists in the SQLite workspaces table with matching slug
No error logged by the handler

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the workspace token creation flow, verifying crypto/rand generation, SHA-256 hashing, DB insertion, and plaintext token returned exactly once.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens with owner API key and optional label and expires fields
real_components: Echo HTTP server with registered routes
Auth middleware (server_foundation spec 01)
POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens handler
Workspace token generator (crypto/rand + base62 encoder)
SHA-256 hasher
SQLite database with workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
mockable: crypto/rand (can be real; mock only for collision testing)
expected_effects: HTTP 201 response returned
Response body contains token (af_wt_ prefixed), token_id (8 base62 chars), label, expires_at, created_at
token field matches regex ^af_wt_[0-9A-Za-z]{8}_[0-9A-Za-z]{32}$
workspace_tokens table row contains SHA-256 hash of secret (not plaintext)
expires_at computed as created_at + 30 days when expires omitted
expires_at is null when expires=0

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for delegated workspace access using a workspace token, verifying middleware validates token, attaches workspace_id to context, handler checks scope, and returns full workspace object.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug with Authorization: Bearer af_wt_<token_id>_<secret> for a valid, non-expired, non-revoked token scoped to that slug
real_components: Echo HTTP server with registered routes
Auth middleware (server_foundation spec 01) performing token lookup, hash verification, expiry/revocation checks, and context attachment
GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug handler
SQLite database with workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
expected_effects: HTTP 200 response with full workspace object (all eight fields)
Response is identical to what owner/admin callers receive (no redaction)
HTTP 403 returned when same token is used for a different slug
HTTP 401 returned when token is expired or revoked

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the workspace token revocation flow, verifying auth, authorization, DB update, idempotency, and information hiding for cross-workspace token IDs.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id with owner API key for a valid token belonging to the workspace
real_components: Echo HTTP server with registered routes
Auth middleware (server_foundation spec 01)
DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/tokens/:token_id handler
SQLite database with workspaces and workspace_tokens tables
expected_effects: HTTP 204 with empty response body on first call
HTTP 204 with empty response body on second call (idempotent)
workspace_tokens row has non-null revoked_at after first call
HTTP 404 returned when token_id belongs to a different workspace (information hiding)
HTTP 404 returned when slug does not exist
HTTP 403 returned for non-owner caller

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for admin listing all workspaces, verifying the admin credential is accepted and all workspace records are returned ordered correctly.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send GET /api/v1/workspaces with Authorization: Bearer af_at_<admin_token>
real_components: Echo HTTP server with registered routes
Auth middleware (server_foundation spec 01)
GET /api/v1/workspaces handler
SQLite database with workspaces table containing records owned by multiple users
expected_effects: HTTP 200 with bare JSON array containing all workspace records from all users
Array ordered by created_at ASC with id ASC as tiebreaker
Each object has all eight workspace schema fields
HTTP 403 returned if same request is made with a workspace token
Empty array returned when no workspaces exist in the database
